stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Os Lusíadas was an epic poem popular in sixteenth-century Portugal.
  • The poem celebrated a mythologized vision of Portuguese maritime exploration around an undiscovered “Cape of Storms.”
  • That same century, Portuguese navigators reached a “cape” off the southern tip of Africa.
  • They named it the Cape of Good Hope after a hopeful detail in the poem.
  • The “cape” was actually a peninsula now known as the Cape Peninsula, which extends southward from the city of Cape Town.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the role a misconception played in the naming of a place. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Thinking it was a cape, Portuguese navigators named a peninsula the Cape of Good Hope after a detail in a popular poem.
The poem Os Lusíadas celebrated a mythologized vision of Portuguese maritime exploration.
Extending southward from Cape Town lies the Cape Peninsula, originally called the Cape of Good Hope after a detail in a poem.
In the sixteenth century, Portuguese navigators reached a feature off the southern tip of Africa and named it the Cape of Good Hope.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It emphasizes the role of a misconception (navigators believed the peninsula was a cape) in the naming of the place.

Choice B describes the poem without addressing the naming. Choice C notes the original name but doesn’t emphasize the navigators’ misconception. Choice D states when navigators named the feature but doesn’t emphasize the misconception.
